text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, little Luna decided she wanted an owl sweater. There weren't many silversmiths in her village, but she was determined. She visited an old cottage tucked between pine trees. There lived an owl puppeteer named Old Hazel.

"Hazel! Wouldn't you weave something for our little owl?" Luna asked.

"Aweigh a wee owl? Sure thing," Hazel replied with a chuckle. "But owls take time."

So Hazel spun something soft, brown, and beautiful. It wasn't life-size, but Luna loved it. They called it Orion. Orion wore an owl hat and had big bright eyes.

Every night at dusk, Luna stole Orion off Old Hazel's branch. They went on adventures under the stars.

One night, a hawk swooped down.

"Luna, fly away!"

"No way!"

She flew around. The hawk took off.

Next morning, Hazel found Orion.

"Luna loves her owl."

"Oh, I know," Luna said softly
